title: mitracarpus scaberulus
intro: Mitracarpus scaberulus is a flowering plant species in the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the "canela brava" or "wild cinnamon". It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. The leaves and stems of Mitracarpus scaberulus can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
